mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a win rate below 45%, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 205 on the CPI index, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and economic momentum, which immediately boost market sentiment and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls fall below 137,000 thousand, severe job losses trigger recession fears and force rapid portfolio de-risking, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The market rallies with positive 20-day returns in >50% of months where PAYEMS drops below the threshold, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and increasing unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that severely damages corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising rates and unemployment fails to predict negative returns with a win rate below 40%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth slows below 0.5 percent, weak macroeconomic expansion reduces earnings growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5%, or the GDP threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward tests.
